title: Executive Assistant (3 Months Contract)
description:
• Broad scope covering executive assistance , investor interactions, and projects.
  • Good exposure within a global investment firm.

About Our Client

A well-known global investment firm with a strong presence across private equity, credit, and real assets.

Job Description

  • Review and screen incoming mail and emails, compose, draft, proofread, edit, and finalise correspondence, including emails and formal documents
  • Plan and coordinate logistics for domestic and international travel, including detailed itineraries, agendas, directions, contingency materials, and timely, accurate expense reporting
  • Manage and monitor Executives' calendars, proactively anticipating scheduling conflicts, meeting requirements, and communication needs; promptly communicate any changes
  • Schedule and coordinate meetings and executive events, including notifying attendees, reserving meeting rooms, arranging required equipment/materials, and coordinating hospitality services
  • Coordinate investor and portfolio company meetings, including preparation of materials, tracking action items, and managing follow-ups
  • Manage logistics for internal and external meetings such as board meetings, investor presentations, and industry events
  • Proactively anticipate executives' needs and take initiative to ensure effective management of schedules and priorities
  • Liaise professionally and courteously with portfolio companies, investors, guests, and employees
  • Provide administrative assistance for deal execution and firm-wide initiatives, including special projects and event planning as required
  • Review and screen incoming mail and emails, compose, draft, proofread, edit, and finalise correspondence, including emails and formal documents
  • Plan and coordinate logistics for domestic and international travel, including detailed itineraries, agendas, directions, contingency materials, and timely, accurate expense reporting

The Successful Applicant

  • Has 10 years of secretarial experiences within financial services firms.
  • Possesses excellent business acumens, stakeholder management and organisational skills
  • Strong time management skills, high level of discretion, integrity and attention to detail
  • Ability to anticipate needs, adjust responses accordingly, take initiative, and work under pressure.
  • Thrive in fast-paced environment and multi-tasks.
  • Proficient in MS Office and experience with CRM and document management systems a plus.

What's on Offer

This role offers exposure to close partnership with executives, and opportunities to build strong stakeholder management and execution skills across high-impact projects.
